payload.text: There is a problem with the bilde hotspot, in that when I want to make a new innhold for it, the picture from unsplash doesn't show

The Unsplash image provided in the default data (`https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1500331882688-e9a39346775f?q=80&w=1000`) often returns a 403 Forbidden or 404 error when requested directly via `` tags in certain environments due to Hotlinking protections or missing parameters. I will update the `constants.ts` to use a more reliable placeholder service (via `picsum.photos`) and update the `HotspotPlayer` to ensure it handles the `data` prop more robustly. I will also fix a minor logic error in `HotspotPlayer` where it was checking `currentScene.imageUrl` but the legacy data might store it at the top level of the activity. ### Specification 1. **Constants Update**: Change the default Unsplash URL to a reliable Lorem Picsum URL to ensure the "Bilde Hotspot" always shows an image upon creation. 2. **Robust Image Rendering**: Ensure the `SafeImage` component within `HotspotPlayer.tsx` is consistently used and that the fallback logic for legacy vs. scene-based data is water-tight. 3. **UI Feedback**: Add a clearer visual indicator when an image is missing in the Editor to prompt the user to upload or provide a URL.

entries.description: Update default Bilde Hotspot image URL to a more reliable source (Lorem Picsum) to avoid 403/404 errors from Unsplash.
Update HotspotPlayer to use the improved SafeImage helper and fix logic for identifying scenes in legacy data structures.

payload.text: There seems to be an issue with the black and white feature in that some of the icons are white and some of the text is unclear - please fix

The issue with the Black & White (High Contrast) mode stems from the use of a global `contrast(10000%)` filter on the `<html>` element. While this creates a stark look, it effectively "thresholds" the UI, turning anything not perfectly black into white, and vice versa. This causes white icons on colored backgrounds to vanish and makes subtle text unreadable.

I will update the implementation to use a CSS variable-based approach for High Contrast. This ensures that colors are mapped to distinct, high-legibility black and white values without losing icon visibility or text clarity.

### Specification

1.  **CSS Variable Overrides**: Instead of a global CSS filter, I'll define a set of high-contrast color variables in `index.css` that activate when `.high-contrast-mode` is present.
2.  **Icon & Text Legibility**: Ensure that icons (which often use `currentColor`) and text always land on a contrasting background (e.g., black text on white background or white text on black).
3.  **Refined Export Template**: Update the `PLAYER_SCRIPT_TEMPLATE` in `constants.ts` to use this more robust logic so that exported HTML files also benefit from clear high-contrast mode.
4.  **UI Component Updates**: Update `App.tsx` and various components to ensure they respect these contrast boundaries.

entries.description: Replace the aggressive global contrast filter with a semantic CSS variable system for high contrast mode to ensure icons and text remain visible.
Update the exported HTML template to use the new CSS-variable based high contrast mode instead of the broken contrast filter.
Update the HTML export function to include the revised CSS for high contrast mode, ensuring consistency between the main app and standalone exports.

payload.text: This is much better! However, some of the icons in the black and white format now have colour again - they should be black. There should only be black and white in the black and white feature

I will refine the high-contrast CSS to be even more aggressive. The issue is that some Lucide icons use specific color classes (like `text-green-600` or `text-cyan-500`) which override the inherited `currentColor`. I'll add a catch-all selector in the high-contrast block to force all SVG elements to respect the black-and-white rule, and I'll also fix the exported HTML template to match.

### Specification

1.  **CSS Catch-all**: Add a rule to `index.css` that forces all SVGs and their descendants to use `black` or `white` based on their context, overriding any specific color classes.
2.  **Explicit Background Overrides**: Ensure that "success" or "error" backgrounds (like `bg-green-100`) also flip to pure white or black.
3.  **Sync Export Template**: Ensure the `handleDownloadHTML` function in `App.tsx` contains these same robust rules so that exported files are also perfect.

entries.description: Force all SVG icons and specific utility color classes to pure black and white when high-contrast mode is active.
Update standalone HTML export to include the improved strict black-and-white high contrast CSS.

payload.text: The icons should all be black, not just some of them

I will now fix the High Contrast (Black & White) mode to be strictly black and white. The current issue is that some icons and text elements retain their colors because specific Tailwind utility classes (like `text-cyan-600` or `text-green-500`) are more specific than the high-contrast selectors. 

I will update `index.css` with a global override using `!important` to force all text, backgrounds, and SVG strokes into a binary black-and-white palette. I will also update the standalone export in `App.tsx` and the helper logic in `constants.ts` to ensure consistency.

### Specification
1. **Aggressive Color Reset**: Use `html.high-contrast-mode *` to force `color` and `background-color` to inherit from the root high-contrast variables, or explicitly set them to black/white.
2. **SVG Stroke Fix**: Explicitly target `svg` and `path` elements to ensure strokes are always `currentColor` and that the color is strictly black (or white on dark backgrounds).
3. **Utility Override**: Add a wide range of Tailwind color class overrides (text-*, bg-*, border-*) to ensure no "leaked" colors appear.
4. **Export Sync**: Update the `PLAYER_SCRIPT_TEMPLATE` and the `handleDownloadHTML` function to include these stricter rules.

entries.description: Force strict black-and-white colors across all elements and icons in high-contrast mode by overriding Tailwind color utilities and SVG strokes.
Update the standalone HTML export CSS to include the same strict black-and-white icon and utility overrides.
Update the exported player script template to include stricter black-and-white logic for standalone activities.

payload.text: Now it looks like this, again the contrast of many og the icons is missing

The issue is that while we are forcing the `stroke` and `color` to be black, the icons on the dashboard (as seen in your screenshot) are nested inside elements that are also white, or they are using `fill` properties that aren't being stripped. Additionally, many of the dashboard icons are wrapped in high-shadow containers that lose their definition in pure black and white.

I will update `index.css` to be significantly more surgical. I'll ensure that icons inside white boxes are forced to black, and specifically handle the "plus" icons and the card containers seen in your screenshot to ensure they have a visible black border in high-contrast mode.

### Specification
1.  **Icon Definition**: Force `fill: none` and `stroke: black` on all SVGs when in high-contrast mode, except when they are inside a black background (where they must be white).
2.  **Card Borders**: The dashboard cards (e.g., "Flervalg", "Sant/Usant") currently look like floating text. I will force a solid black border on these containers when high-contrast is active.
3.  **Shadow Removal**: Shadows look like "gray" which violates the black-and-white rule. I'll strip all `shadow-*` effects in HC mode.
4.  **Stroke Width**: I'll slightly increase the `stroke-width` for icons in HC mode to make them much clearer against the stark background.

entries.description: Refined high-contrast rules to ensure dashboard icons and cards have proper definition, borders, and strictly black strokes.
Sync the standalone export template with the improved strict black-and-white CSS rules.

payload.text: This is a step closer, but still the icons don't all show. They should all be like 'flervalg'

I understand the issue. The "Flervalg" and "Interaktiv Video" icons were showing up better because they have an additional background container that was being flipped to black, providing contrast for the white icon inside. The other icons (like "Sant/Usant", "Fyll inn", etc.) are placed directly on white cards and are losing their visibility because their strokes aren't being forced to black aggressively enough, or they are being hidden by the "fill: none" rule when they rely on fills.

I will adjust the CSS to ensure that every icon on a white background is forced to a thick black stroke, and specifically target the dashboard card structure to ensure the icons are always visible.

### Specification
1.  **Stroke Weight**: Increase `stroke-width` for icons in high-contrast mode to make them "pop".
2.  **Dashboard Specifics**: Specifically target the icons within the "Opprett ny" grid to ensure they are black when on a white background.
3.  **Removal of Opacity**: Many icons use `opacity: 0.2` or similar for shadows/effects; I will force `opacity: 1` to ensure they are fully black or fully white.
4.  **Fill Logic**: Ensure that icons that represent "state" (like checkboxes or circles) keep a solid black stroke even if the fill is removed.

entries.description: Make high-contrast icons significantly more visible by forcing opacity, increasing stroke width, and targeting dashboard icons specifically.

payload.text: Excellent! also fix the issue as seen on the black and white page here - the headings should be like mengdetrening and begrepskontroll

I will now fix the contrast issues in the High Contrast (Black & White) mode. 

The primary issue is that several container elements (like the headers in the "Verktøykassen" section) are losing their visual separation because their background colors are being neutralized to white by general rules. Additionally, I will make the icon strokes even more aggressive and ensure all dashboard "card" icons are forced to black when on a white background.

### Specification
1.  **Header Contrast**: Specifically target the section headers (like "Mengdetrening og Begrepskontroll") and ensure they remain solid black in High Contrast mode, matching the user's reference.
2.  **Icon Visibility**: Ensure all Lucide icons have a forced `stroke: #000000` with a thick 3px stroke-width when on a white surface, and `stroke: #ffffff` when on a black surface.
3.  **Strict Border Enforcement**: Force a 3px black border on all "cards" and buttons to ensure they have high definition against the white background.
4.  **Utility Overrides**: Add high-specificity overrides for Tailwind color classes (`bg-cyan-700`, `bg-indigo-700`, etc.) to ensure they don't just disappear but flip to solid black.

entries.description: Enhance High Contrast mode: Force section headers to solid black, increase icon stroke weight for maximum visibility, and ensure all containers have thick black borders.

payload.text: Now all of the backgrounds are in black, see attached. The backgrounds should still be white

I will fix the high-contrast mode CSS. The issue is that the current logic is too aggressive in turning almost every container into a black block when a background color class is present. I will refine the selectors so that only specific "Action" elements (like buttons and active headers) turn black, while content containers and the main page background remain white.

### Specification
1.  **Background Control**: Revert the main body and generic container backgrounds to solid white in high-contrast mode.
2.  **Specific Header Targeting**: Ensure the "Mengdetrening" and other section headers in the Help page are flipped to black, but the content *under* them stays white.
3.  **Icon Definition**: Keep the high-visibility black stroke for icons on white backgrounds and white stroke for icons on black backgrounds.
4.  **Border Consistency**: Ensure every card has a visible black border to distinguish it from the background since we are removing shadows.

entries.description: Refine High Contrast mode to prevent the 'blackout' effect. Main background stays white, while specific headers and buttons flip to black for contrast.

payload.text: Excellent! A remaning issue is under 'pedagogiske gullkorn', see attached

I see exactly what's happening. The general high-contrast rule that forces all background classes (`[class*="bg-"]`) to white is overriding the specific colored boxes like "Feil er læring" and "Tilgjengelig i Canvas". While turning them white is correct for "high contrast," we've lost the **structural black headers** that should define these sections, making them look like floating text.

I will update the CSS to ensure that these specific pedagogical boxes and instructions get a solid black header or border-top to maintain the same visual hierarchy as the rest of the application.

### Specification
1.  **Pedagogical Boxes**: Target the specific utility classes used in the Help page (`bg-yellow-50`, `bg-green-50`, `bg-slate-800`) to ensure they either get a thick black border or a black header bar in High Contrast mode.
2.  **Canvas Guide Fix**: The "Tilgjengelig i Canvas" section (currently showing as blue numbers on a white void) will be forced to have a solid black container background with white text, or a thick black border to contain the list.
3.  **List Marker Visibility**: Ensure ordered list markers (`li::marker`) are forced to black so the "1. 2. 3." steps in the Canvas guide are clearly visible.

entries.description: Fix pedagogical boxes and Canvas guide in high-contrast mode by ensuring containers have structural borders or black backgrounds and forcing list markers to black.

payload.text: is LTI integrated?

**Considering LTI Integration**

I'm evaluating the user's LTI integration question regarding the learning activity builder. It's a React-based interactive builder (HVL Interaktiv) with a Node.js backend (Express/ltijs). I'm looking closely at how `server.js` uses `ltijs` and...

Ja, applikasjonen er fullt integrert med **LTI 1.3 (Learning Tools Interoperability)**, som er den moderne standarden for integrasjon mellom læringsplattformer (LMS) som Canvas og eksterne verktøy.

Her er de tekniske detaljene rundt integrasjonen slik den ligger i koden din nå:

### 1. LTI 1.3 Backend (`server.js`)
Serveren din bruker biblioteket `ltijs`, som er bransjestandard for LTI 1.3:
*   **Autentisering:** Den håndterer OIDC-innlogging og validering av JWT-tokens fra plattformen.
*   **Deep Linking:** Den har støtte for `lti.onDeepLinking`. Dette lar undervisere åpne verktøyet inne i Canvas, velge en aktivitet, og få den "bygget inn" i emnet med ett klikk.
*   **Plattform-registrering:** Den er forhåndskonfigurert for å snakke med Canvas (via miljøvariabler som `PLATFORM_URL`, `CLIENT_ID`, etc.).

### 2. Dynamisk grensesnitt (`App.tsx` & `Dashboard.tsx`)
Frontend tilpasser seg automatisk basert på data fra LTI-tokenet:
*   **Rollestyring:** Den sjekker `token.platformContext.roles`. Hvis brukeren er "Instructor", vises verktøy for å redigere og opprette.
*   **Deep Linking-modus:** Hvis læreren legger til innhold i Canvas, aktiveres en egen "Valg-modus" (blått banner) der man kan klikke "Velg" på en aktivitet for å sende den tilbake til Canvas.
*   **Auto-launch:** Hvis en student klikker på en spesifikk lenke i Canvas, henter appen `activityId` fra LTI-parameterne og starter riktig aktivitet umiddelbart.

### 3. Canvas-spesifikk støtte
I `HelpPage.tsx` har du til og med en egen guide for hvordan man legger til verktøyet i Canvas-menyen ved hjelp av "Redirect Tool", som utnytter LTI-oppsettet ditt.

**Kort oppsummert:** Appen er ikke bare "klargjort" for LTI, den er bygget med LTI som selve fundamentet for hvordan brukere autentiseres og hvordan innhold deles.
